topics: 🐂 ובעל השור נקי — what the owner is exempt from
🪨 Bull rubbing on a wall — intent vs accident in capital cases
🏛️ Bulls without owners: women, orphans, hekdesh, ger
💀 Dedicating a bull condemned to stoning

overview.title: 📖 Overview — Killer Oxen, Slaves, and Owner-less Bulls
overview.label: The sugya:
overview.text: Daf 22 sweeps through three Mishnayot at the seam between Chapter 4 and Chapter 5. It works out what the owner is 'free' from when his bull kills, when intent matters, who counts as an 'owner' for liability, and what happens to a bull condemned to stoning whose verdict is reversed.
overview.hebrewSource: וּבַעַל הַשּׁוֹר נָקִי. נָקִי מִדְּמֵי עֶבֶד. נָקִי מִדְּמֵי וְולָדוֹת.

slides.title: 🧱 What 'ובעל השור נקי' Excludes
slides.subtitle: The Yerushalmi maps competing readings of the exemption clause.

boxes.title: 👤 נקי מדמי עבד
items: Owner is exempt from paying for a slave
Even though a slave normally has 30 sela liability
Verse derives from 'כי ינצו אנשים' — people, not bulls

boxes.title: 👶 נקי מדמי ולדות
items: Owner is exempt from paying for fetuses
Bulls that act unintentionally are exempt
Even if intentional — still no fetus payment

boxes.title: 📜 Slave damages source
items: או בן יגח או בת יגח
Two opinions on extent of payment

boxes.title: 💭 The dispute
items: R' Chiyya bar Va: addition can't exceed core
Other view: pays the entire damage
R' Yasa heard a teaching from R' Shmuel bar Yitzhak but forgot

callout.label: Key move:
callout.text: The same three words 'ובעל השור נקי' generate two distinct exemptions depending on which baraita you follow.

slides.title: 🪨 Bull Rubbing on a Wall
slides.subtitle: When the death looks accidental, when does liability still attach?

nodes.content: משנה: שור שהיה מתחכך בכותל ונפל על האדם
ואיש כי יכה כל נפש אדם — to include lethal blow
Reish Lakish: R' Hoshaia explained — Mishnah is about כופר after death.

callout.label: Yerushalmi twist:
callout.text: Resh Lakish reframes the Mishnah: it isn't about court execution at all — it's about the kofer payment owed once the victim has died.

slides.title: 💬 Bulls Without Owners — Who Pays?
slides.subtitle: The Mishnah lists six 'ownerless' bulls. R' Yehudah carves out three.

lines.speaker: M
lines.text: Mishnah: שור האשה, שור היתומים, שור אפיטרופוס, שור המדבר, שור ההקדש, שור הגר שמת — all subject to death penalty.

lines.speaker: RY
lines.text: R' Yehudah: the wild bull, the hekdesh bull, and the heir-less ger's bull are exempt — מפני שאין להם בעלים.

lines.speaker: RH
lines.text: R' Hoshaia: for damages R' Meir holds liable, R' Yehudah exempt; but everyone agrees on כופר — liable.

lines.speaker: RY2
lines.text: Some say (R' Yochanan in the name of R' Yannai): just as they argue about damages, they argue about kofer too.

callout.label: Practical ruling:
callout.text: R' Zeira tells R' Yirmiyah: follow R' Hoshaia — disputed in damages, agreed in kofer.

slides.title: 📊 The Sale and the Reversed Verdict
slides.subtitle: Two adjacent cases on a goring ox: bad sale and reversed sentencing.
headers: Shmuel / R' Yochanan vs Reish Lakish
rows: Sold a bull, turns out to be a gorer
Shmuel: seller can say 'I sold it for slaughter'
Bull goes out to be stoned, witnesses are zomemim
R' Yochanan: first to grab acquires it
Reish Lakish: ייאוש טעות — false despair, doesn't transfer ownership
Slave going to execution, witnesses zomemim
R' Yochanan: he acquires himself; R' Lakish: false despair

callout.label: Important:
callout.text: R' Yochanan and Reish Lakish disagree about whether despair based on a false premise still effects ownership transfer.

slides.title: 🧠 Dedicating a Stoned Bull
slides.subtitle: When does hekdesh take effect on a bull condemned to die?

steps.content: 1️⃣ Bull rubs on a wall and kills — sentence not yet pronounced.
2️⃣ Before sentencing: dedication is valid; if slaughtered, meat is permitted.
3️⃣ After sentencing: dedication invalid; if slaughtered, meat is forbidden.
4️⃣ Sentence creates a status of איסור הנאה that blocks normal acquisition.
5️⃣ If verdict is reversed: R' Yochanan vs Reish Lakish on whether ownership reverts.

callout.label: Bottom line:
callout.text: The legal effect of a death-sentence is so strong that even hekdesh cannot override it once the verdict is final.

summary.principle: Daf 22 maps the boundaries of liability for killer oxen: who counts as an owner, what the exemption verse excludes, and how a death-sentence reshapes ownership.

flowSteps.content: Each derashah shapes a different facet of bull-owner liability.

🎓 Key Principle:
Owner liability is bounded by both verse and ownership status — and the death-sentence locks ownership in place.

quiz.q: What does 'ובעל השור נקי' exclude according to one tannaitic reading?
quiz.e: One reading derives 'נקי מדמי עבד' — exempt from paying for a slave even though slave damages normally apply.

quiz.q: What does Reish Lakish say R' Hoshaia explained about 'shor mit'chakhech bakotel'?
o: It's about a tame bull only
It only applies in a public domain
quiz.e: Reish Lakish: R' Hoshaia, the father of the Mishnah, taught that this Mishnah is about the kofer payment after death.

quiz.q: According to R' Yehudah, why are the hekdesh and ger's bulls exempt from death?
o: Because the verse only mentions tame bulls
Because they have no owners — אין להם בעלים
Because they are not in a settled area
quiz.e: R' Yehudah derives from 'והועד בבעליו' that an animal needs an owner to be subject to the death penalty.

quiz.q: What does Rav say about a sold bull that turns out to be a gorer?
o: It is מקח טעות — a mistaken sale
quiz.e: Rav: it's a mistaken sale; Shmuel: the seller can claim he sold it for slaughter.

quiz.q: What is the key dispute between R' Yochanan and Reish Lakish about a bull going to be stoned with zomemim witnesses?
o: Whether the bull can be slaughtered
Whether 'first to grab acquires' or it's יאוש טעות
quiz.e: R' Yochanan: first to grab acquires; Reish Lakish: it's despair based on a mistake and doesn't transfer ownership.
